July 17, 2002 Greetings from Download.com!No software developer is immune to bugs. Even the cleanest codewill have to go through a debugging process, but some debuggersare more efficient than others. Check out Download.com'scomplete collection of debugging tools here, and put away thecan of Raid.Also this week, we've got some very handy utilities. First,there's Chilkat, a cool zip/unzip ActiveX control that you canadd to any language with ActiveX support (which is most majorones). But my personal favorite is VB-PowerWrap, a simple toolthat takes all the far-flung bits and pieces of your Visual Basicmasterpiece and socks them all away into one executable filefor ease of distribution.Becky Waring

Achievingpersistence with Entity BeansEnterprise JavaBeans (EJB) allow for greater code reuse and flexibility. See how Entity Beans enable you to leverage EJB functionality in your apps.

Countinglines of code can help measure progressOne developer has come around to thinking that counting lines of code can yield useful information. He explains why and shares a sample app that automates the process.in ActiveX Chilkat Zip 6.0.2Need to add zip capability to your apps? Create and decompress zip archives with the Chilkat Zip ActiveX control. The control provides easy access to individual files stored within a zip archive. You can search for a file within the zip using a wildcard string and then extract individual files or files matching a pattern. Files can be unzipped to any directory, and the control's memory management allows any size file to be compressed or decompressed in a constant memory footprint. The control allows you to compress directly from memory buffers or strings, as well as add multiple files with wildcarding. There's even a real-time progress monitor for zip and unzip operations. Chilkat supports Active Server Pages, Visual Basic, Visual C++, Delphi, C++ Builder, and all languages that use ActiveX controls.in JavaAnfy Java 2.0Anfy is a collection of 52 applets for Web pages, desktop wallpapers, or screensavers. Within the program's simple interface, you can quickly apply real-time graphic effects to GIF and JPEG images. Effects include 3D texture-mapped cube menus, lake effects, water and fire, deformations, zoom rotators, lens magnification, waving flags, animations, scrolling text, customizable IFS fractals, color changers, light shading, tunnels, and wormholes.in Tools & EditorsVB-PowerWrap 3.1Distribute Visual Basic applications the easy way with VB-PowerWrap. VB-PowerWrap embeds all dependent and non-dependent files into the executable of your VB program, including DLLs and OCXs, graphics, databases, and text files, to create a single clickable application. VB-PowerWrap scans a VB project, wraps the appropriate files, and creates a self-contained file for distribution. In addition, VB-PowerWrap compresses all DLLs and OCXs and registers appropriate controls on the user's machine if needed. It works with Visual Basic 4, 5, and 6. Secrets of Invalidating Tax Assessments June 18,"UNCOVER THE STRATEGIES, TACTICS AND REMEDIES IN INVALIDATING TAX ASSESSMENT Hereunder is the list of our Upcoming Training Program: How to Prepare Performance Evaluation Forms and Performance Improvement Plans (PIP) - June 17 Reg. Link: https://forms.gle/wynM5hZ7zsYFGgJo7 Invalidating Tax Assessments – June 18 Reg. Link: https://forms.gle/n3JRJHB4jGqjybWP8 Supervisory Leadership Development – June 20 Reg. Link: https://forms.gle/YfuXNAhKHumQAVut5 Modern Procurement and Supply Management – June 25 AM Reg. Link: https://forms.gle/X3umJ9houjAsh18n9 Warehouse Management Best Practices – June 25 PM Reg. Link: https://forms.gle/nKyTrPLLUmeNqaDq8 Work Attitude and Values Enhancement – June 27 AM Reg. Link: https://forms.gle/tEuhroo2PV11TPaG9 Accounting For Non-Accountants – June 27 PM Reg. Link: https://forms.gle/mndSfjB3kXSWmVJU8 Invalidating Tax Assessments UNCOVER THE STRATEGIES, TACTICS AND REMEDIES IN INVALIDATING TAX ASSESSMENT June 18, 2025 2:00pm - 5:00pm *LIVE VIA ZOOM* Introduction: Are you or is your business being assessed (or likely to be assessed) by the B.I.R.? Have you received any notice (Letter of Authority, Tax Verification Notice, Subpoena Duces Tecum, and the like)? Are you prepared? You need to be aware of what exactly you need to do or you will stand to spend an arm and a leg. Uncover the strategies, tactics, and remedies in handling tax assessments, disputes, and claims for refunds. Course Objectives: This course is designed specifically for individuals and businesses currently facing tax assessments by the Bureau of Internal Revenue (B.I.R.) or those who anticipate an impending assessment. Participants will gain practical insights into strategies for addressing tax assessments, contesting erroneous claims, and pursuing tax refunds. By the end of the course, participants will be empowered to effectively manage and navigate their tax challenges, minimizing financial and legal risks. Major Takeaways: Develop strategies to respond to B.I.R. assessments and potential disputes. Recognize critical assessment stages and appropriate reactions to notices received. Identify common audit findings and learn how to effectively challenge them. Understand the tax refund process and the necessary steps for successful claims. Course Content Assessing Your Tax Assessment Situation a. Legal basis of authority to assess Taxpayer b. Prescriptive period of Tax Assessments * 3 years vs 10 years prescriptive period * Extension of period to assess through the issuance of waiver c. Types of BIR audit/investigation notices * Letter of authority, Letter Notice, Tax Verification Notice and Mission Order * Service of assessment notices d. Immediate Steps to Take Upon Receiving a Notice * Key actions to undertake to protect your interests. e. Submission of documents pursuant to BIR notice * Issuance of subpoena duces tecum * Application of the best evidence rule Understanding the Stages of Tax Assessment a. Notice of discrepancy (NOD) b. Preliminary Assessment Notice (PAN) Best practices for responding to a PAN to avoid a final assessment. c. Final Assessment Notice (FAN) What to do when you receive a FAN and how to build your case. d. Final Decision Disputed Assessment (FDA) Strategies for navigating the final resolution process and implications for your business. Challenging Common Audit Findings a. Questioning the validity of tax assessment at different stages of tax assessment Overview of frequent discrepancies found during audits and how they affect your assessment. b. Addressing factual and legal issues in tax assessment Crafting compelling arguments and gathering supporting documentation to challenge the assessment. Claiming Tax Refunds a. Understanding Refund Claims and Their Requirements Types of claims: Erroneously paid taxes versus overpaid input VAT. Identifying the prescriptive periods for claims and key documentation requirements. b. Rule on exhaustion of administrative remedies Knowing the procedural steps necessary to pursue your rights effectively. Featured Speaker: MR. SENEN QUIZON Tax Partner - Deloitte Philippines Senen has over 17 years of professional experience in tax services. His experience covers tax ruling assistance, tax investigation assistance, tax training, and tax consultancy for companies engaged in the Manufacturing, Life Sciences & Health Care, and Technology, Media & Telecommunications industries. Have you received any notice (Letter of Authority, Tax Verification Notice, Subpoena Duces Tecum, and the like)? Are you prepared? You need to be aware of what exactly you need to do or you will stand to spend an arm and a leg. Uncover the strategies, tactics, and remedies in handling tax assessments, disputes, and claims for refunds. Course Objectives: This course is designed specifically for individuals and businesses currently facing tax assessments by the Bureau of Internal Revenue (B.I.R.) or those who anticipate an impending assessment. Participants will gain practical insights into strategies for addressing tax assessments, contesting erroneous claims, and pursuing tax refunds. By the end of the course, participants will be empowered to effectively manage and navigate their tax challenges, minimizing financial and legal risks. Major Takeaways: Develop strategies to respond to B.I.R. assessments and potential disputes. Recognize critical assessment stages and appropriate reactions to notices received. Identify common audit findings and learn how to effectively challenge them. Understand the tax refund process and the necessary steps for successful claims. Course Content Assessing Your Tax Assessment Situation a. Legal basis of authority to assess Taxpayer b. Prescriptive period of Tax Assessments * 3 years vs 10 years prescriptive period * Extension of period to assess through the issuance of waiver c. Types of BIR audit/investigation notices * Letter of authority, Letter Notice, Tax Verification Notice and Mission Order * Service of assessment notices d. Immediate Steps to Take Upon Receiving a Notice * Key actions to undertake to protect your interests. e. Submission of documents pursuant to BIR notice * Issuance of subpoena duces tecum * Application of the best evidence rule Understanding the Stages of Tax Assessment a. Notice of discrepancy (NOD) b. Preliminary Assessment Notice (PAN) Best practices for responding to a PAN to avoid a final assessment. c. Final Assessment Notice (FAN) What to do when you receive a FAN and how to build your case. d. Final Decision Disputed Assessment (FDA) Strategies for navigating the final resolution process and implications for your business. Challenging Common Audit Findings a. Questioning the validity of tax assessment at different stages of tax assessment Overview of frequent discrepancies found during audits and how they affect your assessment. b. Addressing factual and legal issues in tax assessment Crafting compelling arguments and gathering supporting documentation to challenge the assessment. Claiming Tax Refunds a. Understanding Refund Claims and Their Requirements Types of claims: Erroneously paid taxes versus overpaid input VAT. Identifying the prescriptive periods for claims and key documentation requirements. b. Rule on exhaustion of administrative remedies Knowing the procedural steps necessary to pursue your rights effectively. Featured Speaker: MR. Din stemme teller – Coop Valget 2026 er i gang!,"Coop Election 2026 is underway!
Now you have the opportunity to influence the future of Coop Øst SA.
The candidates are ready – who will represent you and the members?

Are you interested in grocery retail and Coop as a local retailer in your market area? Use your vote in the election of member representatives at the Cooperative's Annual Meeting and Regional Council. Your vote helps shape the future of your cooperative.
Follow the link in the email – you will be taken directly to the voting page.
Review the candidates and cast your vote. You can cast additional votes.
Click ""Continue"" when you are finished - your vote is then registered.
What does your voice go to?

As a member of Coop, you can help influence – because Coop is a bit of yours! In Coop Valget, you vote on which members will represent you at the Annual Meeting and the Regional Council of your cooperative.

At the Annual Meeting, the representatives discuss important issues that will affect Coop in the future, such as annual accounts, long-term plans, investments and the election of the board. The Regional Council is also a forum where members can provide input on the development of the business.
Your voice helps shape the future of your cooperative.
Member benefits | My Page | Contact us

Re: server is rejecting mail from single domain,"NOW I get it, I thought that postfix was complaining because host lookup of the FQDN does not match the IP. But to be honest, it looks to me like someone has owned their server and having a bit of fun, because the server is literally announcing itself as ""Spam-Server"" in the HELO instead of the FQDN! $ telnet 64.22.240.195 25 Trying 64.22.240.195... Connected to 64.22.240.195. Escape character is '^]'. 220 Spam-Server Microsoft ESMTP MAIL Service, Version: 6.0.3790.1830 ready at Fri, 8 Feb 2008 16:55:39 -0800 Sandy Drobic wrote: > Jonathan Dill wrote: >> James D. Parra wrote: >>> I can suggest that to him, but this is a client so I can only ask. >>> From the >>> client's vantage point, our server is the only one rejecting his >>> mail, so I >>> need to tread politely. >>> >>> Concerning removing reject_non_fqdn_hostname (or >>> reject_non_fqdn_helo_hostname) is not something I want to do. If this >>> weren't a client of ours I wouldn't worry too much about it, but it >>> is and I >>> must. Is there a way could add an exception to the main.cf for this >>> domain >>> or IP address? >>> >> >> If he sent e-mail to me, my server would also reject it, then you >> would not be alone. But seriously, I wonder if setting up >> /etc/nsswitch.conf to check ""files"" first and adding an entry to >> /etc/hosts would fix it e.g. >> >> 64.22.240.195 ns.ky3.com >> >> I am not sure how postfix verifies fqdn, if it uses the OS resolver >> stack or some other method. > > It is not the client hostname that is rejected, it's the helo name > that the client itself determines to announces itself to the receiving > server. > > This is especially stupid since you have to configure the helo of your > own system. > > So you can only kick the admin of the server with the non-fqdn-helo or > whitelist the client. >",0,phishpuzzer,"Re: server is rejecting mail from single domain

But to be honest, it looks to me like someone has owned their server and having a bit of fun, because the server is literally announcing itself as ""Spam-Server"" in the HELO instead of the FQDN! $ telnet 64.22.240.195 25 Trying 64.22.240.195... Releasing the accelerator program,"Dear valued community members,
Are you ready to take part in the next phase of Ripple's growth? We've just launched the RippleNet Accelerator Program, a 300M initiative aimed at accelerating volume and utility for XRP.
To participate, simply log in to your XRP Toolkit account and follow the instructions to claim your share of the program. It's that easy!

We believe that our members are the key to our achievements, and we're committed to building a strong and supportive community that values private key ownership and encourages participation in the Ripple ecosystem.
Join us and be a part of our journey to revolutionize the world of cryptocurrency. Thank you for your continued support.

Protect Against Fraud and ID Theft,"Security Center 128-bit encryptionAt PayPal, we want to increase your security and comfort level with every transaction. From our Buyer and Seller Protection Policies to our Verification and Reputation systems, we'll help to keep you safe. We recently noticed an attempt to log in to your PayPal account from a foreign IP address and we have reason to belive that your account was used by a third party without your authorization. If you recently accessed your account while traveling, the unusual log in attempts may have been initiated by you. Therefore, if you are the rightful account holder, click on the link below to log into your account and follow the instructions. Click This Link To Follow The Instructions If you choose to ignore our request, you leave us no choice but to temporarily suspend your account. If you received this notice and you are not the authorized account holder, please be aware that it is in violation of PayPal policy to represent oneself as another PayPal user. Such action may also be in violation of local, national, and/or international law. PayPal is committed to assist law enforcement with any inquires related to attempts to misappropriate personal information with the intent to commit fraud or theft. Information will be provided at the request of law enforcement agencies to ensure that impersonators are prosecuted to the fullest extent of the law. Thank you for your patience as we work together to protect your account. Sincerely, PayPal Account Review Department PayPal, an eBay Company *Please do not respond to this e-mail as your reply will not be received.",1,phishyai-phishing,"Protect Against Fraud and ID Theft
